summary: EXP-162 delivers five patch-ready prose additions to SEC-1.9 addressing ISS-983 (major — apparent-path sufficiency defender escape) and ISS-985 (moderate — n(r) is radial, not altitudinal). P1 softens the TLDR to remove the disputed 'physical overlap not optical coincidence' framing and anchors it to multi-station scaling. P2 inserts a new 'What the critique does and does not claim' paragraph that classifies eclipse predictions into three classes — concedes single-observatory case (WIN-010 on Chapman grounds, not refraction grounds) and anchors the critique onto class-(ii) multi-station scaling (WIN-025 9-station 2024, WIN-044 FSF Aug 2026, WIN-068 39-eclipse ensemble). P3 adds the 'shadow path is horizontal, not vertical' paragraph converting the radial-n(r) observation into a strengthener for the per-station scaling problem. P4 replaces the angular-diameter paragraph with the over-determined-historical-constraint framing (Espenak & Meeus). P5 replaces the bottom line with WIN-039 mention + August 2026 falsification challenge. Defense-survives drops from 4 (curmudgeon's rating) to 2 (residual moves either concede the critique or break a different section). No verdict changes. Newsworthy flag set: August 2026 FSF methodology challenge.
decider_notes: Section 1.9 currently lives in part1 html (not part1b — the expansion note clarifies this). ISS-985 is marked assigned_to=EXP-162 in open-issues.json; ISS-983 is assigned-analyst without the assigned_to field but was routed to EXP-162 per the 14:32 decider run note. Both issues are addressed by this single expansion. The separate follow-up issue raised by the curmudgeon — Section 1.5 still carries stale 'r = 14,105 km, n ≈ 1.83' — is explicitly out of scope for EXP-162 (noted in cross_references.known_stale_text_in_other_sections) and should remain as a separate issue the decider files.
